reason: The only residual finding is a minor omission in the 取り下げ explanation. The information an agent would most need — that full error list display IS available — is already captured in the 実装済み section as "エラーメッセージの全件表示". The missing nuance only affects the explanation of *why* the layout-preserving variant was withdrawn, not the availability of the feature itself. An agent answering practical implementation questions would not be misled.

findings_assessment.justification: The knowledge file's 実装済み section already lists 'エラーメッセージの全件表示', so the key fact (full error list display is provided) is accessible to the agent. The missing nuance is the clarifying note inside the 取り下げ entry that explains the partial availability. This would only matter if a user specifically asked about the withdrawn requirement's scope; for normal usage questions about error display, the agent has sufficient information. Phase E did not resolve this because the generated content in the fix phase was identical to the Phase D version — the 取り下げ bullet was left unchanged, indicating the fix loop exited without actually applying the correction to that specific line.
